question: What is the energy source of the massive orbital platform orbiting planet Xylos?

answers.ground_truth: The massive orbital platform known as the 'Aegis Station' draws its power from a captured and contained miniature black hole.
answers.original: The Aegis Station, the massive orbital platform orbiting planet Xylos, is powered by a captured miniature black hole.
answers.perturbed: The Helios Array draws its power from a massive solar sail that taps into the planet's magnetic field.

ops.description: Molten-Streams can reach temperatures of several thousand degrees.
Chef Zorgon is known for using Phlorian Salt in their culinary creations.
The Chrono-Regulator emits a Faint Hum when active.<SEP>The Chrono-Regulator emits a faint hum when active.
Aegis is the second planet in the Lumina System, serving as the mining hub.<SEP>Aegis is the second planet in the Lumina System, rich in heavy metals and serving as the system's mining hub.
Triskelion has three moons in a stable figure-8 orbit.

entities.name: Power Distribution
entities.description: The generated power from the Stellar Converter is distributed across the planet Xylos.

entities.name: Helios Array
entities.description: The Helios Array is an orbital platform that draws its power from a massive solar sail tapping into the planet's magnetic field.

entities.name: Aegis Station
entities.description: The Aegis Station is a large orbital platform positioned above the planet Xylos, dedicated to planetary defense and equipped with powerful energy shields.<SEP>Aegis Station is a large orbital platform positioned above the planet Xylos, dedicated to planetary defense and powered by a captured miniature black hole.

entities.name: Solar Sail
entities.description: The Solar Sail is a device used by the Helios Array to tap into the planet's magnetic field for power.

entities.name: Binary Star
entities.description: The binary star's radiation is converted into solar power by the Solar Matrix.

entities.name: Solar Matrix
entities.description: The Solar Matrix is designed to capture and convert radiation from a binary star into solar power.

relations.description: The generated power from the Stellar Converter is distributed across the planet Xylos.
The Helios Array draws its power from a massive solar sail that taps into the planet's magnetic field.
The Aegis Station is positioned above the planet Xylos to provide planetary defense.<SEP>Aegis Station is positioned above the planet Xylos to provide planetary defense.

The Aegis Station uses a miniature black hole as its primary power source, requiring containment fields to maintain stability.<SEP>Aegis Station draws its power from a captured and contained miniature black hole.

The Aegis Station relies on containment fields to maintain the stability of the miniature black hole, which is its most critical task.<SEP>Aegis Station requires constant energy input to maintain the containment fields for the miniature black hole.

The Aegis Station utilizes Hawking radiation from the miniature black hole to meet its immense power requirements.<SEP>Aegis Station utilizes Hawking radiation from the miniature black hole for its power requirements.
The Aegis Station houses a large crew responsible for its operation and maintenance.
The Aegis Station employs cutting-edge technology for containing black holes, distinguishing it from other orbital platforms.
The Aegis Station serves as a primary defense point, distinguishing it from other orbital platforms that use different power methods.
Aegis Station serves as a primary defense point for the planet Xylos.
Aegis Station utilizes cutting-edge technology for containing and utilizing the power of a miniature black hole.
The Solar Matrix is designed to capture and convert radiation from the binary star into solar power.<SEP>The Solar Matrix captures and converts radiation from the binary star into solar power.

entities.name: Aethelite
entities.description: Aethelite is a shimmering, crystalline substance found in rare geological formations, known for its lightness, brittleness, and interaction with ambient light.<SEP>Aethelite is a brittle element found in remote locations, known for its interaction with ambient light and luminescence storage.
